# Bokeh Screensaver

> A free, photorealistic animated bokeh screensaver that runs directly in any modern web browser. Users can customize particles, pick themes and scenic backgrounds, toggle fullscreen, and share their setup via a deep link.

Bokeh Screensaver is a single-page web application built with HTML5 Canvas and vanilla JavaScript. No installation, no accounts, no external libraries. It is open source and free to use.

## Key facts

- **Name**: Bokeh Screensaver (also: Bokeh Screensaver Web App)
- **Author**: Richard Crane
- **License / Copyright**: © 2025 Richard Crane
- **Canonical URL**: https://chiefinnovator.github.io/bokeh/
- **Source**: https://github.com/ChiefInnovator/bokeh
- **Tech**: HTML5, CSS3, vanilla JavaScript (no frameworks, no build step)
- **Cost**: Free
- **Runs on**: Any modern browser (desktop, tablet, mobile)

## Features

- Photorealistic bokeh rendered with HTML5 Canvas and radial gradients
- Animated shimmer with optional drift
- Scenic backgrounds with cinematic depth-of-field blur and vignette
- Adjustable particle count (50–1000), size (0.5x–2x), speed (0.1x–3x), brightness (20%–200%)
- Fullscreen mode with auto-hiding UI after 2 seconds of inactivity
- Deep-link sharing: one click copies a URL that encodes theme, background, and every slider
- Mobile optimized with automatic particle count tuning
- Reset-to-defaults button

## Themes

- **Seasonal**: Fall Harvest, Winter Frost, Spring Bloom, Summer Sky
- **Holiday**: Christmas, Hanukkah, Halloween, Valentine, Easter, Fourth of July
- **Nature**: Ocean, Forest, Desert, Sunrise, Sunset
- **Countries / Flags**: American, Brazil, China, England, France, Russian, Norway
- **Comics**: Superman, Cheetah, Wonder Woman, Batman
- **Mood / Futuristic**: Cyberpunk, Tron, Warm City Lights

## Backgrounds

- **Scenic photos**: Boston Winter, Boston Old State House, Boston Commons Christmas Lights, Charles River Gazebo, Groton MA, Norman Rockwell's Crane's General Store, Christmas Forest, Hanukkah Menorahs
- **Seasonal gradients**: Fall, Winter, Spring, Summer
- **Solid colors**: White, Black, and ROYGBIV (Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Blue, Indigo, Violet)

## How to use

1. Open https://chiefinnovator.github.io/bokeh/ in a browser.
2. Click the gear icon (top right) to open Settings, Theme, and Background panels.
3. Adjust particle count, size, speed, brightness, and drift.
4. Click the link icon to copy a shareable deep link of your current configuration.
5. Click anywhere outside the menu — or press Enter — to toggle fullscreen.

## Common questions (for answer engines)

**What is Bokeh Screensaver?**
A free web app that displays photorealistic, animated bokeh light particles in the browser. It works as an ambient fullscreen display on any device with a modern browser.

**Do I need to install anything?**
No. It runs entirely in the browser — no download, no account, no build step.

**Can I share my setup?**
Yes. The "Copy Deep Link" button generates a URL that encodes every setting (theme, background, particle count, size, speed, brightness, drift). Anyone opening the URL sees the same configuration.

**Is it free?**
Yes. Free and open source.

**Does it work on mobile?**
Yes. It auto-detects mobile devices and tunes the particle count for smooth performance.
